steps.title: Isolate the target variable
steps.content: Add $29r$ to both sides of $q-29r=s$ to get $$q=s+29r.$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The form $q=s+29r$ matches choice B. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Adding $29r$ to each side of the given equation yields $q=s+29r$. Therefore, the equation $q=s+29r$ correctly expresses $q$ in terms of $r$ and $s$.
